user.title: XCC Heat Pump Controller Setup
user.description: Configure your XCC heat pump controller connection

data_description.ip_address: The IP address of your XCC controller
data_description.scan_interval: How often to update data from the controller in seconds (10-3600, default: 120)
data_description.entity_type: Choose how entities are created: MQTT (via MQTT broker) or Integration (direct Home Assistant entities)
data_description.regenerate_entity_ids: Leave unchecked to keep any existing entity IDs and their recorder history when re-adding the integration. Tick only to rewrite legacy IP-based entity IDs (e.g. sensor.stav_jednotky_192_168_0_50_*) to the canonical xcc_* form — this will reset history for renamed entities.

error.cannot_connect: Failed to connect to XCC controller. Please check the IP address and network connection.
error.invalid_auth: Invalid username or password. Please check your credentials.
error.timeout: Connection timeout. The controller may be unreachable or overloaded.

abort.already_configured: This XCC controller is already configured.

init.title: XCC Controller Options
init.description: Configure advanced options for your XCC controller

data.regenerate_entity_ids: Regenerate entity IDs on next reload

data_description.scan_interval: How often to update data from the controller in seconds (10-3600, default: 120)
data_description.regenerate_entity_ids: Rename any legacy entity IDs (e.g. containing the controller IP address) to the canonical xcc_<name> form and drop duplicates. Runs once on the next reload; the toggle is cleared afterwards. Warning: recorder history is keyed by entity_id, so renamed entities start with fresh history.

set_parameter.name: Set Parameter
set_parameter.description: Set a parameter value on the XCC controller

refresh_data.name: Refresh Data
refresh_data.description: Force refresh of data from the XCC controller
